creator: jeremie@le-hen.org
text:
--zhXaljGHf11kAtnf
 Content-Type: text/plain; charset=us-ascii
 Content-Disposition: inline
 
 Hi,
 
 On Thu, Aug 27, 2009 at 07:04:37AM +0000, Jeremie Le Hen wrote:
 > 
 > >Submitter-Id:	current-users
 > >Originator:	Jeremie Le Hen
 > >Organization:	None
 > >Confidential:	no
 > >Synopsis:	[ports] New opt-in knob to compile ports with SSP
 > >Severity:	non-critical
 > >Priority:	low
 > >Category:	ports
 > >Class:		change-request
 > >Release:	FreeBSD
 > >Environment:
 > System: FreeBSD felucia.tataz.chchile.org
 > 
 > >Description:
 > 	The first patch add a knew knob WITH_SSP_PORTS that, if enabled,
 > 	will add -fstack-protector to CFLAGS.  I've chosen this name to
 > 	not conflict with WITH_SSP that affects src/ build.
 > 
 > 	This is a great win in term of security and follows the trail
 > 	of FreeBSD-8.0 which is now compiled with SSP (opt-out).
 > 
 > 	Although most of the ports build smoothly with this knob turned on,
 > 	few of them won't build out of the box.  With kan@'s recent change
 > 	to put SSP symbols in libssp_static.a instead of libc,
 > 	-fstack-protector must be provided explicitely at link time.
 > 	lang/perl-5.* for instance have to be slightly modified to be built
 > 	with SSP as it does not pay attention to LDFLAGS.
 > 	You will find another patch attached for lang/perl* as well.
 > 
 > 	I don't have the power and time necessary to test and fix every
 > 	ports, but given this is an opt-in I think we could live with this
 > 	and let patches trickle in the ports tree.
 
 He are updated patches.  Moreover they will be downloadable directly
 from GNATS instead of inlined in the PR.
 
 This time, I've Cc'ed portsmgr@, in the hope to reach a decision on how
 to merge this.  There are clearly a lot of ports broken by this commit,
 but I think it would be overly difficult to find and fix them all before
 committing this.  On the other hand, it is a pity impede the use of the
 stack-protector for the majority of ports that already works correctly.
 
 I know there is no branch in ports/ as in src/ to test new features, but
 certainly you have already been faced to such a problem in the past.
 How did you manage this?  Is it possible for instance to add the option
 without documenting it officially, so only informed user will try it?
 
 Regards,
 -- 
 Jeremie Le Hen
 
 Humans are born free and equal.  But some are more equal than others.
 					    Coluche
 
 --zhXaljGHf11kAtnf
 Content-Type: text/x-diff; charset=us-ascii
 Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="WITH_SSP_PORTS.patch"
 
 Index: Mk/bsd.port.mk
 ===================================================================
 RCS file: /mnt/repos/freebsd-cvsroot/ports/Mk/bsd.port.mk,v
 retrieving revision 1.643
 diff -u -p -u -p -r1.643 bsd.port.mk
 --- Mk/bsd.port.mk	15 Jul 2010 14:48:50 -0000	1.643
 +++ Mk/bsd.port.mk	28 Jul 2010 18:56:09 -0000
 @@ -1655,6 +1655,12 @@ CFLAGS:=	${CFLAGS:C/${_CPUCFLAGS}//}
  .endif
  .endif
  
 +.if defined(WITH_SSP_PORTS)
 +SSP_FLAGS?= -fstack-protector
 +CFLAGS:= ${CFLAGS} ${SSP_FLAGS}
 +LDFLAGS:= ${LDFLAGS} -fstack-protector
 +.endif
 +
  .if defined(WITH_DEBUG) && !defined(WITHOUT_DEBUG)
  STRIP=	#none
  STRIP_CMD=	${TRUE}
 
 --zhXaljGHf11kAtnf
 Content-Type: text/x-diff; charset=us-ascii
 Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="WITH_SSP_PORTS_lang_perl.patch"
 
 Index: lang/perl5.10/Makefile
 ===================================================================
 RCS file: /mnt/repos/freebsd-cvsroot/ports/lang/perl5.10/Makefile,v
 retrieving revision 1.121
 diff -u -p -u -p -r1.121 Makefile
 --- lang/perl5.10/Makefile	15 Jul 2010 14:48:51 -0000	1.121
 +++ lang/perl5.10/Makefile	28 Jul 2010 18:57:29 -0000
 @@ -90,6 +90,10 @@ STRIP_CMD=	${TRUE}
  CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	-Doptimize="${CFLAGS}"
  .endif
  
 +.if defined(WITH_SSP_PORTS)
 +CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	-A append:ldflags=-fstack-protector
 +.endif
 +
  .if defined(ENABLE_SUIDPERL) || defined(WITH_SUIDPERL)
  CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	-Dd_dosuid=define
  PLIST_SUB+=		SUIDPERL=""
 Index: lang/perl5.12/Makefile
 ===================================================================
 RCS file: /mnt/repos/freebsd-cvsroot/ports/lang/perl5.12/Makefile,v
 retrieving revision 1.122
 diff -u -p -u -p -r1.122 Makefile
 --- lang/perl5.12/Makefile	23 Jul 2010 17:41:03 -0000	1.122
 +++ lang/perl5.12/Makefile	28 Jul 2010 18:58:00 -0000
 @@ -88,6 +88,10 @@ STRIP_CMD=	${TRUE}
  CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	-Doptimize="${CFLAGS}"
  .endif
  
 +.if defined(WITH_SSP_PORTS)
 +CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	-A append:ldflags=-fstack-protector
 +.endif
 +
  .if defined(WITH_GDBM)
  CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	-Di_gdbm
  LIB_DEPENDS+=		gdbm.3:${PORTSDIR}/databases/gdbm
 Index: lang/perl5.8/Makefile
 ===================================================================
 RCS file: /mnt/repos/freebsd-cvsroot/ports/lang/perl5.8/Makefile,v
 retrieving revision 1.111
 diff -u -p -u -p -r1.111 Makefile
 --- lang/perl5.8/Makefile	15 Jul 2010 14:48:51 -0000	1.111
 +++ lang/perl5.8/Makefile	28 Jul 2010 18:57:48 -0000
 @@ -92,6 +92,10 @@ STRIP_CMD=	${TRUE}
  CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	-Doptimize="${CFLAGS}"
  .endif
  
 +.if defined(WITH_SSP_PORTS)
 +CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	-A append:ldflags=-fstack-protector
 +.endif
 +
  .if defined(ENABLE_SUIDPERL) || defined(WITH_SUIDPERL)
  CONFIGURE_ARGS+=	-Dd_dosuid=define
  PLIST_SUB+=		SUIDPERL=""
 
 --zhXaljGHf11kAtnf
 Content-Type: text/x-diff; charset=us-ascii
 Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="WITH_SSP_PORTS_databases_db41.patch"
 
 Index: Makefile
 ===================================================================
 RCS file: /mnt/repos/freebsd-cvsroot/ports/databases/db41/Makefile,v
 retrieving revision 1.65
 diff -u -p -u -p -r1.65 Makefile
 --- Makefile	13 Jun 2010 12:01:22 -0000	1.65
 +++ Makefile	28 Jul 2010 21:54:53 -0000
 @@ -32,6 +32,10 @@ CONFIGURE_ARGS=	--enable-compat185 --ena
  		--includedir=${PREFIX}/include/db41
  USE_LDCONFIG=	yes
  
 +.if defined(WITH_SSP_PORTS)
 +CONFIGURE_ENV+=	LDFLAGS=-fstack-protector
 +.endif
 +
  .if !defined(NOPORTDOCS)
  INSTALL_TARGET=	install install_docs
  PORTDOCS=	*
 
 --zhXaljGHf11kAtnf--
